body.title: Red/green queue gate — circuit breaker for worker-daemon
body.result: cmd_next + gate file + auto-trip at 3 timeouts + worker_daemon self-restart via exec; 9 tests all pass
body.description:
Queue has no circuit breaker — timed-out sprints reset and immediately get reclaimed, causing daemon spin loops. Also: daemon uses old claim process (list|grep) and won't self-regulate even when a gate is tripped.

Three-piece implementation:
1. `cc_queue.py next` command — returns highest-priority unclaimed sprint ticket respecting a gate file (~/.TheIgors/cc_channel/queue_gate.json). When gate is tripped, returns nothing. Auto-trips after 3 consecutive timeouts on the same ticket.
2. `worker_daemon.sh` updated to call `cc_queue.py next` instead of manually filtering list output. When `next` returns nothing (gate tripped or queue empty), daemon sleeps and retries.
3. Daemon self-restart on script update — at top of main loop, check if worker_daemon.sh has been modified since last iteration; if so, `exec bash "$0"` to reload. This lets T-queue-gate take effect without manual daemon restart.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (next command + gate logic + timeout counter), lab/claudecode/worker_daemon.sh (call next, self-restart check)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; gate file is JSON flat-file in cc_channel/; timeout counter in ticket metadata; self-restart uses exec not subshell
**Scope boundary:** In scope: next command, gate file, auto-trip at 3 timeouts, daemon uses next, daemon self-restart. Out of scope: UI for gate state, manual pause/resume CLI commands (V2), per-worker routing in next.
**Test plan:** test next returns highest-priority sprint ticket; test next returns nothing when gate file present; test auto-trip writes gate file after 3 timeouts on same id; test daemon self-restart: modify script mtime → next loop iteration calls exec
